summary: This cluster focuses on the taxonomic documentation and biodiversity assessment of freshwater green algae, specifically desmids, in the Garhwal region of Uttarakhand, India. The research identified 42 taxa across seven genera, including nine new species, collected from relatively pristine water bodies within a 2360 km² area in the foothills of the Western Himalayas. Key findings emphasize the high biodiversity value of these habitats and the importance of their preservation, contrasting their ecological condition with more disturbed urban environments. The geographic scope is narrowly confined to this Himalayan region, with the work contributing foundational species inventories for this understudied area.
